transcript: Hey! Hey, question: I heard you got some scammers calling your phone; is that true?

I’m Officer Perkins with Baton Rouge City Police. I talked to your son. Your son called me. I’m Officer Perkins with Baton Rouge City Police. He let me know that you had some scammers calling your phone; is that true?
You’re goin’ so fast. You know, I’m 94 and a half. Go slow down. What is your name?
No, sir! I don’t live in your building. I’m a police officer!
Oh, you’re a police officer! Okay! What do you wanna ask me?
He said that you had scammers calling your phone.

That is correct! And a dozen of times or maybe more, it's criminals—that they calling me; they wanna take care of my Medicare and I’m gonna get $50 a month—all kinds!—my Amazon, you know, somebody took $1,595.37. All the time, the same thing! I’m 72 years right here in Los Angeles. I never did have any credit cards—
—and by 94 I’m not gonna take it!
—Hey, Bolden. It's Perkins. I got it—
So, my son Jimmy called you?
Okay. So, you know, I had a—a guy, seven feet tall, you know—black guy—following my granddaughter and she was going, you know, from our praying place—

—And he followed her. And she made a mistake by going into her apartment that I gave her. And he started to try to get into the window! And my f—my man, you know, that works there with his girlfriend for twelve years, he seen it and he—he knows my granddaughter, knows her husband, knows the baby. And he said, “What are you doin’ here?” He said, “I’ve got my girlfriend here. She’s waitin’ for me.” “The hell, she’s waitin’ for you!” he said—
—“I know her husband and everything. Now lemme see. I’m gonna call the police right now.”—
—Wheeler. I got it. Please hang up—
—the minute he said he called the police, he—he—he took off. He took off in a minute.
—You can hang up. I got it.
—Hey, Wheeler. It’s Off—it’s PD-70. I got it. You can hang up. It’s gonna be a 6-8.
[in background] Well, I tried to find a place—
Wait a minute. I don’t know. I—you’re talkin’ to him or to me? Talking to my si—
—Sir, are you still on the phone?—
—yeah, I’m still on the phone. Is my son still there with you?—
Okay. No. I think he hung up. This is you an I—
So, what are you doin’? If you’re a police officer you wanna re—report this in, uh, to the police?
Yeah, I want you—I wanna know what you got goin’ on.
—my grandson that lives there, you kno—
—my grandson who lives there in the apartment seven.—
—Pueblo Police. Dispatch. This is Skyler. How can I help you?
Hey, Skyler. This O—this is PD-70. I—I don’t know what’s goin’ on with his phone, but I have it. And PRU, you can hang up—
—Oh, okay. [unclear] Oh, hold—hey, hold on, I’m gonna—hold on one second. One second. Hold on, please. Please. Hello?
Baton Rouge City Police. This is Wheeler.
Wheeler. Hey, e—you can hear me?—
[in background to unknown caller] To hell with you! You’re a sonofabitch.
Okay, I think we got a 68. So you can hang up; I got it—
—I just hung up on [unclear] I’m gonna—
—Three or four people talking on here?
Yeah, it’s like three people talkin’ right now—
PRU, I have the call. The—it’s a—it’s a 68. I have it; y’all can hang up.
Do you have that Jakob was supposed to—he just came from the Army, three and a half years. He left—was in the United States Army, you know? Graduated with honor—
So, he came yesterday and I gave him the signature that he’s gonna report that to the police—with my signature—that nobody—no stranger should be trespassing my property—
—Hello, this is the City of [unclear] Police.
—I’m very happy you did get it, yes!
Alright! Well, anything else I can help you with?
And—and—well, what can I tell you? It looks like we’re gonna have to put bars up. It’s gonna cost me a lotta money!
Right. And that’s in West Hollywood, right?—
—We gonna have to put bars up, you know, in his apartment upstairs and in her apartment downstairs—

—you know—put ‘em in there. So, the problem is, I’ve been 72 years right here in Los Angeles. And what happens is, when they do a robbery—anything, you know—they get out of jail in—in day or two—they are out of there!—
—That’s the problem we have in here! If you would’ve told me that 72 years ago, I would say,—
—“This could happen in any other place. But not in America.”
—Pueb—[breaks up]—patch. Skyler. Can I help you?—
So, thank you for your work! God bless you! And you have a great day! You wanna know something else from me or—or this is it?
Uh, this is Pueblo PD. Is there an emergency?
What do you mean? Are you talkin’ to me? Are you still having questions for me? If you still wanna ask me questions—whatever you wanna know—I’ll be happy to help you.
Well, didn’t they tell you? I mean a tall black guy, you know, was following my granddaughter and then she made a mistake by walkin’ into her apartment, the other day—
And—and yesterday he came. And he started to go—
He came yesterday and was gonna break the window and go into the apartment. Good thing that my tenant—the next door—
—that he lives there for me—for twelve ye—this is [redacted].
—Now, when my— [redacted] Avenue, West Hollywood. It’s 9-double oh-4-6, the area code.
No-no. What Seven? I said [redacted]! What “Seven-Zero”?
—Pueblo Police Dispatch. This is Skyler. How can I help you?
I’m sorry, Skyler. I’m talkin’ to a caller on this line—
Let’s—let’s talk to one person. I—I can’t talk to two; I’m ninety-four years of age.
Our phone. Yes, sir. Okay. I’m still tryin’ to get the address from you because your accent is kinda thick for me; so I’m tryin’ ta understand, okay?—

—No, because—because I’m—m—my teeth are now—I have false teeth and everything. So let me—let me give you the addre—address—accent again? I mean the address! It’s [redacted]—can ya understand me here?— [redacted] Avenue.
Can I talk to the—yes, it is!—
Can you [unclear]. I don’t have a—
Okay. Can I s—okay, is this in Baton Rouge? I don’t have a “[redacted] Avenue”—
—Pueblo this is [unclear]. How can I help you?
—pissed off! I don’t know why it’s ringin’—
—to you. This is—this is Bolden with City Police—
It’s in West—It’s West Hollywood and the ar—
It’s not a state! It’s West Hollywood—Los Angeles—California! 90036!
Listen—listen, you’re in Los Angeles, California? You’re speaking to Baton Rouge, Louisiana! That’s the problem!—
—That’s the problem. I need you to hang up and dial—I need you to hang up and dial 911 from wherever you are. Dial “9-1-1”. Okay?—
—I’m not in emergency! I am in my house!—
—I don’t live there! I don’t even live there!
Okay, sir, I’m—sir, I’m in Louisiana. You need to hang up and dial “911”.
—nobo—nobody is there now! Nobody is there!—
—Whatever you need, you need to dial 911. I’m in Louisiana!—
